title: Fixed Number Portability
text:
The JCRA in conjunction with the Guernsey Competition & Regulatory Authority (
GCRA
)are jointly initiating a project to explore the technical and commercial feasibility of delivering fixed number portability (
FNP
) across the Channel Islands. This was identified as a follow on project as part of the JCRA's Telecoms Market Review completed in 2025.
The GCRA and JCRA have jointly appointed Cenerva Limited as an independent consultants to support this project.

---

## 2026-02-27-t-147-fixed-number-portability-case-opening-information-note

Case T-147
Fixed Number Portability: Case
Opening Information Note
Document No: JCRA 26/12 Date: 27 February 2026
Jersey Competition Regulatory Authority
2nd Floor Salisbury House, 1-9 Union Street, St Helier, Jersey, JE2 3RF
Tel 01534 514990
Web: www.jcra.je

Information note
The Jersey Competition Regulatory Authority (the JCRA) in conjunction with the Guernsey Competition
& Regulatory Authority (the GCRA) are jointly initiating a project to explore the technical and
commercial feasibility of delivering fixed number portability (FNP) across the Channel Islands.
FNP gives consumers – both residential and business – the ability to change their provider of fixed
voice services while keeping their existing telephone number (in the case of Jersey, the numbers
starting 01534).
As part of its Telecoms Market Review Final Decision, issued on 16 July 2025, the JCRA stated that it
would initiate a follow-on project to implement FNP.1 The JCRA notes the importance of FNP allowing
consumers to exercise greater choice as to their fixed voice service provider and as an enabler for
future development of broadband line only services.
The GCRA and the JCRA have jointly commissioned Cenerva Limited (Cenerva) as independent
consultants with extensive experience in delivering number portability projects globally to support this
project.
As a starting point, Cenerva, the GCRA and the JCRA will be reaching out to key stakeholders to brief
them on the project and to schedule information gathering sessions. This will be followed by formal
information requests as necessary. Taking into account the findings of this information gathering
phase, and subject to the GCRA and JCRA determining that it is appropriate to proceed, it is intended
that a Draft Decision will be issued in Q2 2026. This will set out the GCRA and the JCRA’s proposals
supported by assessment of the technical and commercial feasibility. These proposals will include
proposed modifications to existing telecoms licences in both Islands in order to bring in FNP.
If there are any queries on this case, please contact robin.hogge@jcra.je.
1 See: T-083 - Telecoms Market Review
